triples.object: Working capital ( WC ) is a financial metric which represents operating liquidity available to a business, organisation, or other entity, including governmental entities. Along with fixed assets such as plant and equipment, working capital is considered a part of operating capital. Gross working cap

triples.subject: Equipment financing
Line of credit
triples.object: A line of credit is a credit facility extended by a bank or other financial institution to a government, business or individual customer that enables the customer to draw on the facility when the customer needs funds. A financial institution makes available an amount of credit to a business or consu

triples.subject: Business plan
triples.object: A business plan is a formal written document containing the goals of a business , the methods for attaining those goals, and the time-frame for the achievement of the goals. It also describes the nature of the business, background information on the organization , the organization's financial projec

triples.subject: Debt financing
Equity financing
triples.object: In finance , equity is an ownership interest in property that may be subject to debts or other liabilities . triples.subject: Crowdfunding
triples.object: Crowdfunding is the practice of funding a project or venture by raising money from a large number of people, typically via the internet. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] Crowdfunding is a form of crowdsourcing and alternative finance , to fund projects "without standard financial intermediaries". [ 3 ] In 2015, over US$

triples.subject: Angel investor
triples.object: An angel investor (also known as a business angel , informal investor , angel funder , private investor , or seed investor ) is an individual who provides capital to a business or businesses, including startups , usually in exchange for convertible debt or ownership equity . Angel investors often pr

triples.subject: Venture capital
Loan application
triples.object: In finance , a loan is the tender of money by one party to another with an agreement to pay it back. The recipient, or borrower, incurs a debt and is usually required to pay interest for the use of the money.

triples.subject: Financial statement
triples.object: Financial statements (or financial reports ) are formal records of the financial activities and position of a business, person, or other entity.

triples.subject: Credit score
triples.object: A credit score is a numerical expression based on a level analysis of a person's credit files, to represent the creditworthiness of an individual. [ 1 ] A credit score is primarily based on a credit report , information typically sourced from credit bureaus .

triples.subject: Collateral
Loan default
triples.object: In finance , default is failure to meet the legal obligations (or conditions) of a loan , [ 1 ] for example when a home buyer fails to make a mortgage payment, or when a corporation or government fails to pay a bond which has reached maturity . A national or sovereign default is the failure or refus

triples.subject: Loan modification
triples.object: Mortgage modification is a process where the terms of a mortgage are modified outside the original terms of the contract agreed to by the lender and borrower (i.e. mortgagee and mortgagor in mortgage states; Trustee and Trustor in Trust Deed states). In general, any loan can be modified, and the pro

triples.subject: Loan servicing
triples.object: Loan servicing is the process by which a company ( mortgage bank , servicing firm, etc.) collects interest, principal, and escrow payments from a borrower. [ 1 ] In the United States , the vast majority of mortgages are backed by the government or government-sponsored entities (GSEs) through purchas

triples.subject: Financial risk
triples.object: Financial risk is any of various types of risk associated with financing , including financial transactions that include company loans in risk of default . [ 1 ] [ 2 ] Often it is understood to include only downside risk , meaning the potential for financial loss and uncertainty about its extent. [

triples.subject: Credit risk
triples.object: Credit risk is the chance that a borrower does not repay a loan or fulfill a loan obligation. [ 1 ] For lenders the risk includes late or lost interest and principal payment, leading to disrupted cash flows and increased collection costs . The loss may be complete or partial. In an efficient market

triples.subject: Interest rate
Borrower
triples.object: A debtor or debitor is a legal entity (legal person) that owes a debt to another entity. The entity may be an individual, a firm, a government, a company or other legal person . The counterparty is called a creditor . When the counterpart of this debt arrangement is a bank , the debtor is more often

triples.subject: Lender
triples.object: A creditor or lender is a party (e.g., person, organization, company, or government) that has a claim on the services of a second party. It is a person or institution to whom money is owed. [ 1 ] The first party, in general, has provided some property or service to the second party under the assumpt

Maria Contreras-Sweet
triples.object: Maria Contreras-Sweet (born 1955) is an American businesswoman and former government official who served as the 24th administrator of the Small Business Administration from 2014 to 2017. triples.subject: New York State Small Business Development Center
triples.object: The New York Small Business Development Centers (NYSBDC) is a partnership program that provides business advising and training to small business owners and entrepreneurs across New York State. triples.subject: United States House Committee on Small Business
triples.object: The United States House Committee on Small Business is a standing committee of the United States House of Representatives . It was established in 1941 as the House Select Committee on Small Business. [ 1 ]

triples.subject: Business.gov
triples.object: Business.gov [ 1 ] is sponsored by the U.S. Small Business Administration to provide small business owners with access to federal, state and local government resources from a single access point.

triples.subject: United States House Small Business Subcommittee on Economic Growth, Tax, and Capital Access

triples.subject: Woman-owned business
triples.object: A woman-owned business is a specific designation used by government agencies and industry associations to identify a business organization owned and operated by female business owners . Most definitions of this term involve a practical look at the legal and ownership structure, as well as the issue

triples.subject: Alabama International Trade Center
triples.object: The Alabama International Trade Center was created in 1979 as one of the first international Small Busicness Development Centers (SBDCs) .  Since its inception, the AITC has provided export trade research, education, finance, and training throughout Alabama .

triples.subject: Business loan
triples.object: A business loan is a loan specifically intended for business purposes. [ 1 ] As with all loans, it involves the creation of a debt , which will be repaid with added interest . There are a number of different types of business loans, including bank loans, mezzanine financing, asset-based financing, i

triples.subject: Small business financing
triples.object: Small business financing (also referred to as startup financing - especially when referring to an investment in a startup company - or franchise financing ) refers to the means by which an aspiring or current business owner obtains money to start a new small business , purchase an existing small bus

triples.subject: MultiFunding
triples.object: MultiFunding is a national business leverage advisor and brokering firm that helps small- and medium-sized businesses across America find debt financing for their companies. MultiFunding has placed over 700 loans in 47 states. It has financing capabilities in excess of $25 million. In addition to co

triples.subject: SBA ARC Loan Program
triples.object: The SBA ARC Loan Program was a United States government program to provide funding for viable small business as part of the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 . The United States Government had allocated Small Business Administration (SBA) backed funds for small businesses in the United

triples.subject: SBA 7(a) Loan
triples.object: The U.S. Small Business Administration 's SBA 7(a) Loan program is the SBA's primary business loan program for providing financial assistance to small businesses . It is designed to expand access to financing to current and prospective American small business owners. The program is so named because

triples.subject: Community Advantage Loan
triples.object: The U.S. Small Business Administration Community Advantage Loan program is designed for new and existing businesses that need loans under $250,000.  The loan can be used to finance a startup company or expand an existing small business or buy real estate.  The SBA guarantees 85 percent of loans up t

triples.subject: OnDeck Capital
triples.object: OnDeck Capital is an American small business lending company with offices in Chicago, IL ; New York, NY ; Denver, CO , and South Jordan, UT . triples.subject: Brock Blake
triples.object: Brock Blake [ 1 ] [ 2 ] is founder and CEO of Lendio , an online marketplace for small business loans. Blake is an Inc. 500 CEO, speaker, and Forbes columnist. [ 3 ]

triples.subject: Opportunity Fund
triples.object: Opportunity Fund is a US-based nonprofit organization that provides financing to underserved small-business owners. [ 1 ]

triples.subject: Christopher Hurn
triples.object: Christopher Hurn is an American writer, entrepreneur, and business executive who works primarily in the field of small business lending . [ 1 ] He is the founder and CEO of Fountainhead, a company that provides commercial real estate financing and growth capital for small business owners. He previou

triples.subject: Lendio
triples.object: Utah-based Lendio [ 2 ] (formerly Funding Universe), [ 3 ] founded in 2011 by Brock Blake and Trent Miskin, is a free online loan marketplace in the U.S. targeting small business owners. [ 4 ]

triples.subject: World Business Lenders
triples.object: World Business Lenders , LLC is an American financial company headquartered in Jersey City, New Jersey with locations in Georgia, California, Connecticut, Florida and Texas. It is a bank service provider and a private direct lender specializing in short-term loans to small and medium-sized businesse

triples.subject: StreetShares
triples.object: StreetShares, Inc. is a financial technology company and small business funding marketplace based outside of Washington, D.C.

triples.subject: SBA 504 Loan
triples.object: The U.S. Small Business Administration 's SBA 504 Loan or Certified Development Company program is designed to provide financing for the purchase of fixed assets , which usually means real estate , buildings and machinery, at below market rates . triples.subject: SAFE-BIDCO
triples.object: State Assistance for Enterprise, Business and Industrial Development Corp , better known as SAFE-BIDCO , was a United States non-depository lender set up by the California State Legislature for financing of small business loans in California. [ 1 ] SAFE-BIDCO was based in Santa Rosa, California [ 2

triples.subject: Enterprise Finance Guarantee
triples.object: The Enterprise Finance Guarantee (EFG) is a UK government -guaranteed lending scheme intended to help smaller viable businesses who may be struggling to secure finance, by facilitating bank loans of between Â£1,000 and Â£1 million.

triples.subject: Microfinance in Tanzania
triples.object: Micro financing in Tanzania started in 1995 with SACCOS (savings and credit cooperative organization) and NGOs.
